wikimediacustomizations-attribution-rest: The Attribution API provides well-structured attribution data for content across Wikimedia projects. For more information about attribution guidelines, expectations, and style guides when reusing Wikimedia content, see the [https://wikimedia-attribution.toolforge.org/attribution-signals/overview.html Attribution Framework].
wikimediacustomizations-attribution-endp: Get attribution information for a page.

Attribution signals are the individual elements that keep Wikimedia projects visible when their content is surfaced in external contexts. Signals provide direct acknowledgment and access to sources, and present additional information that helps end users evaluate and engage with the knowledge they encounter.

Attribution signals may be pulled for any Wikimedia content page, but are most applicable to articles and media files. Not all information will be returned for every type of page; for example, the credit is only returned on media files. To differentiate page type differences from missing data, only fields applicable to the given page type are included in the response, and `null` values are used in cases where data is expected but unavailable.
The schema for the Attribution API.
wikimediacustomizations-attribution-get-: Allows the caller to include additional types of signals in the response, following the [https://wikimedia-attribution.toolforge.org/ Attribution Framework] categories. By default, only `Essential` signals are returned. Additional categories can be specified as a comma-separated list.
The essential information about the page attribution.
The title of the requested page.
The artist or author of the file.
The license of the file.
The URL of the license.
The title of the license.
The link to the page.
The site brand marks of the page.
The name of the site brand mark.
The URL of the site brand mark.
The type of the site brand mark.
The details about the source wiki of the page.
The name of the source wiki.
The type or 'family' of the source wiki.
The ID of the source wiki.
The content language of the wiki site.
The content language of the page.
The trust and relevance information about the page.
The number of contributor counts of the page.
The last modified date of the page.
The number of page views of the page for the last 30 days.
The number of references cited on the page. This property is omitted for file pages and may be null if the count cannot be determined.
The calls to action information for the page.
The donation call to action objects for the page.
The default donation call to action URL.
The foundation donation call to action URL.
The donation call to action URL for the special occasions.
The participation call to action objects for the page.
The talk page call to action URL.
